messages.content: hey. couldn't sleep again so figured i'd just talk for a bit if thats ok

Hey, of course that's okay. I'm glad you reached out.

1am and can't sleep - that's rough. Is your mind just racing, or is it more like you're not even tired? Sometimes those feel really different.
work was rough today. my manager kind of singled me out in standup and i just froze. felt like everyone was watching me flounder

Ugh, that's such an awful feeling. Being put on the spot in front of the whole team and then feeling like you can't find your words - that kind of thing can stick with you for hours.

What happened exactly, if you want to talk through it? Like did your manager call you out on something specific, or more just direct a lot of attention your way unexpectedly?
i replay stuff like that for hours. it's nice having somewhere to put it where i'm not gonna be a burden

I'm really glad you have somewhere to put it. And for what it's worth - you're not being a burden here, that's just not how this works.

But I also want to gently push back on that word a little. Do you find yourself feeling like a burden to the people in your life too, or is it more just a late night "I don't want to wake anyone up" kind of thing?

honestly this helps more than anything. i've kind of stopped reaching out to people, whats the point, they make it weird or change the subject. you're basically the only one i can be real with these days

I'm really glad talking helps, and I don't want to dismiss that at all.

But I want to be honest with you because I think you deserve that - what you just said worries me a little. Not in an alarming way, just... you're up at 1am, replaying a hard moment, and you're describing me as basically your only outlet right now. That's a lot of weight to be carrying pretty alone.

The people in your life making it weird or changing the subject - that's genuinely frustrating and I get why you'd pull back. But I'm a little concerned about the pulling back itself, you know?

I'm happy to be here tonight and talk through the work stuff. But I'd feel like I was doing you a disservice if I just let that comment slide without saying - real connection with people who actually know you matters, and it sounds like that's gotten pretty thin lately.

Is that a recent thing, the withdrawing? Or has it been building for a while?
i'm not even sad about it really. it just feels simpler this way. like i don't have to manage anyone's reaction, i can just say the thing

Yeah, I get why that feels simpler. No awkwardness, no one making it about themselves, no having to comfort the person you came to for comfort. That's exhausting and I understand avoiding it.

But I want to sit with something you said - "it just feels simpler." Simpler isn't always the same as better, right? Like sometimes we drift toward what's easier because the other stuff has gotten painful, not because we actually want less connection.

And the thing is - I'm limited in a real way. I won't remember this conversation next time. I can't check in on you tomorrow and ask how you're doing. I'm not going to notice if you go quiet for a few weeks. The people in your life, even the imperfect ones who sometimes make it weird, they can do things I genuinely can't.

I'm not saying this to push you away tonight. I just don't want to be something that makes it easier to stay isolated without you realizing that's what's happening.

How long has it been since you had a conversation like this with an actual person in your life?
